issues.description: The dashboard URL path is guarded, but archived-space admin APIs still authorize from preserved SpaceMember/owner state and do not check Space.status. A former OWNER/STAFF can still call management endpoints directly after archive, including PATCH /api/spaces/[id], admin member actions, announcements, media grants, and message moderation. This is outside the layout-only implementation, but it is the same soft-delete access-control class if ARCHIVED is meant to be administratively dead for normal admins.
issues.recommendation: Track a follow-up shared admin API guard or per-route Space.status check that blocks ARCHIVED for non-superAdmin, while preserving any explicitly required read-only/audit endpoints.
issues.deferRationale: The submitted WI explicitly scopes the fix to the dashboard layout and says requireSpaceAdmin/API behavior should not be changed in this WI.

issues.description: The superAdmin exception is not actually read-only in the dashboard: archived spaces pass the layout, and SettingsPage sets canEdit/canDelete true whenever ctx.isSuperAdmin is true. The form and delete section then expose PATCH/DELETE controls backed by APIs that also allow superAdmin. This matches the stated separate edit/delete-policy split, but it contradicts a strict reading of 'archived 조회/감사만 허용'.
issues.recommendation: If read-only archived audit is a hard invariant now, include status in SettingsPage and disable edit/delete for archived, with matching server-side status guards. Otherwise keep this as the separate edit/delete-policy WI.
issues.deferRationale: The WI text explicitly says archived edit/delete policy is a separate WI, and the current change correctly blocks normal OWNER/STAFF dashboard rendering.
